reasoning: Libya is deeply divided politically between the Government of National Unity (GNU) in Tripoli and the House of Representatives (HoR) in the east. Recent reports (April 2025) indicate the interim government lacks the authority to ratify international agreements, and any attempt to do so would face severe legal and political obstacles from the HoR. Furthermore, Libya has not yet even submitted its required tariff offers to the AfCFTA Secretariat. With the July 1, 2025 deadline only two months away from the May 1, 2025 knowledge cutoff, there is virtually no functional legislative path or timeframe for Libya to complete the highly technical and politically fraught ratification process.
batch_queries: Libya House of Representatives GNU ratify AfCFTA 2024 OR 2025
